meta.tagline: The premium travel companion — every attraction, hotel and restaurant with maps, QR codes and insider timing.

welcome.editorLetter:
London is not a city you visit. It is a city you rehearse — a rehearsal of centuries, of empires and afternoons, of black cabs and quiet gardens. This companion was built for one purpose: to sit in your pocket and answer the only question that ever matters on a good trip — what should I do next?

Every attraction, hotel, restaurant, hospital and embassy in these pages carries a QR code that opens directly in Google Maps. Every itinerary has a route map, a timeline, and an estimated walking distance. Every chapter has been designed to be beautiful to look at and useful to hold.
welcome.howToUse:
Point your phone camera at any QR code and the location opens in Google Maps in one tap. Every website and booking link is also clickable directly in the PDF. Colour-coded day chapters make navigation effortless.

— The Editors, London Bureau

currency.label: £ Pound Sterling
currency.note: Contactless is king. Notify your bank before travel.

power.label: Type G · 230V
power.note: British three-pin plug. Bring a universal adapter.

sim.label: eSIM or airport SIM
sim.note: EE, Vodafone, Three. eSIM works instantly on arrival.

language.label: English
language.note: Arabic widely understood in Mayfair, Edgware Road.

tipping.note: Check if service charge is already included.

tapWater.label: Safe to drink
tapWater.note: Cafés will refill your bottle on request.

timezone.label: GMT / BST +1
timezone.note: Clocks change last Sunday of March & October.

arrival.title: Border
arrival.desc: Follow signs to UK Border. e-Gates open to most passports incl. GCC. Have your accommodation address ready.

arrival.title: SIM / eSIM
arrival.desc: Activate your eSIM on airport Wi-Fi. Or buy an EE / Three SIM from the arrivals shop (£10 gets you 20GB).

arrival.title: Cash
arrival.desc: Take £50–£100 from an ATM (Halifax / HSBC — never the Travelex booth). Cards work everywhere but keep a note.

arrival.title: Train, not taxi
arrival.desc: Elizabeth Line from LHR T2/T3/T5 to central London: 30 min, £11.50. From LGW take the Gatwick Express: 30 min, £22.

arrival.title: Oyster / Contactless
arrival.desc: Tap your bank card at the gates. It caps at £8.90/day automatically. No Oyster needed for most travellers.

arrival.title: Hotel
arrival.desc: Check-in is usually from 15:00. Most luxury hotels will hold luggage from 09:00 so you can start exploring.

arrival.title: First meal
arrival.desc: Head to Borough Market (Southwark) or Dishoom (Covent Garden). Recover with tea, then a nap. Jet lag rewards discipline.

weatherTable.sweetSpot: May–early June and September deliver the best light, the smallest queues at ticketed attractions and the longest usable daylight. Avoid the last two weeks of August — school-holiday peak — unless you have booked everything in advance.

modes.name: Tube
modes.desc: Fastest across zones 1–2. 11 lines. Daily cap £8.90.

modes.name: Bus
modes.desc: One-hour Hopper: unlimited buses in 60 min for one fare.

modes.name: Elizabeth
modes.desc: New east-west line. Heathrow to Bond St in 32 min.

modes.name: River Boat
modes.desc: Uber Boat by Thames Clippers. Views + no traffic.

modes.name: Walk
modes.desc: Central London is 3 km wide. Everything is walkable.

modes.name: Uber / Bolt
modes.desc: Cheaper than black cab off-peak. Split fares in-app.

oysterTips.title: Contactless bank card
oysterTips.desc: Just tap the yellow reader. Daily cap £8.90 · weekly cap £44.70. Uses your bank's FX rate — usually best.

oysterTips.title: Apple / Google Pay
oysterTips.desc: Same as contactless. Use the same card each journey or your fares won't cap properly.

oysterTips.title: Oyster (visitor)
oysterTips.desc: Only useful if your card charges FX fees. £7 deposit + top up. Refund at any station machine.

oysterTips.title: Under 11s free
oysterTips.desc: Kids travel free on Tube, DLR, bus and Elizabeth Line with an adult. No paperwork needed.

transport.exit6Tip: Use exit 6 at Westminster tube — you emerge with Big Ben framed directly in front of you. Every other exit spits you into traffic. Your first photo of London is worth planning for.

overview.caption: A schematic map — for exact directions, scan any QR code in this guide.
